notes: [assault.objective.activate | presentation] Activate an objective (set health, arm its decreasers + destructibles)  -  ActivateObjective also spawns the objective's waypoint sprite (SpawnObjectiveSprite → WP_AssaultDestroy + health bar) on the server, matching QC's WaypointSprite_SpawnFixed. But there is no client render/networking path, so the sprite is not visible on-screen (see assault.objective.waypoint_sprite). Logic/state is faithful; the visible marker is still absent.

[assault.objective.waypoint_sprite | presentation] Objective waypoint sprites (defend/push/destroy + health bars + radar icon)  -  Presentation only: the objective waypoint sprite is spawned, health-bar-updated, disowned-on-destroy, and ticked SERVER-SIDE (SpawnObjectiveSprite + WaypointSprites.Think; WaypointSprites.Active = Waypoints.cs:244). But there is NO client render/networking path — nothing in src/XonoticGodot.Net reads WaypointSprites to serialize/render it. So the attacker has no on-screen objective marker, health bar, or radar icon yet. The state machine is faithful; the visible pixels are absent.

[assault.mapobject.assault_wall | timing] func_assault_wall cosmetic wall toggles with its objective's health  -  Timing: QC re-evaluates each func_assault_wall on a 0.2s nextthink; the port re-evaluates every tick (DriveWalls from the per-frame DriveFrame) but only re-stamps the edict on a state transition. Functionally equivalent (toggle happens at most ~0.2s sooner); transition-gated and cheap. Not a behavioral divergence.

[assault.objective.destructible_heal | presentation] func_assault_destructible regen / event_heal (walls can be healed back up + sprite update)  -  Presentation: the heal updates the wall's waypoint-sprite health bar server-side (WaypointSprites.UpdateHealth) but, like all the Assault sprites, that bar is not surfaced to the client (no waypoint render/network path). QC's func_breakable_colormod (the visible color tint on a healed wall) is not modeled. The heal LOGIC + health restoration is faithful + live.

[assault.notify.play_hitsound | audio,liveness] Force a hitsound when shelling a func_assault_destructible (PlayHitsound hook)  -  The PlayHitsound handler is registered (Assault.cs:598-599) and its predicate matches QC exactly, but the hook chain is DEAD: MutatorHooks.FirePlayHitsound (MutatorHooks.cs:611) has ZERO callers. The port's hitsound is driven entirely by the STAT(HITSOUND_DAMAGE_DEALT_TOTAL) accumulator (DamageSystem.cs:337-343), which only accrues for IS_PLAYER victims and never consults the PlayHitsound mutator hook. So the assault forced-hitsound on a wall victim never actually plays. Behaviorally parity-neutral (a func_assault_destructible victim isn't a player, so the stat path wouldn't fire a hitsound either), but the code is unreachable.

[bot-ai.think.throttle | values] Per-bot think throttle + god mode + ping (bot_think)  -  Simulated bot ping/aim-latency (CS(this).ping, bot_aimlatency) is not modeled; bots have zero network latency, so their lead/reaction is slightly sharper than stock at low skill.

[bot-ai.aim.lead | logic,values] Projectile lead + ballistic arc (bot_shotlead / findtrajectorywithleading)  -  Ballistic aim is a closed-form gravity-drop approximation, not QC's 10-trace tracetoss search; lobbed-weapon aim (mortar/nade) is less accurate, especially with obstacles in the arc, and never traces the world to confirm the toss lands. (UNCHANGED.); Shot speed is read from g_balance_<wep>_primary_speed/gravity for the single ChosenWeapon (via the BotAimShotSpeed override hook, now used by Devastator/Crylink/Fireball), not per-weaponentity with W_WeaponSpeedFactor applied; weapons without those cvars are treated as hitscan (no lead). (PARTIALLY NARROWED.)

[bot-ai.target.chooseenemy | logic] Enemy selection (nearest attackable with LOS, sticky tracking, SUPERBOT rating)  -  No second see-through-transparent (DPCONTENTS_OPAQUE) scan pass and no misc_breakablemodel secondary targeting; bots won't shoot breakables or acquire targets through transparent surfaces. (UNCHANGED.)

[bot-ai.weapon.choose | logic,values] Weapon selection by range + combos (havocbot_chooseweapon)  -  Weapon picking is a hitscan-at-range / splash-up-close bucket choosing the highest-impulse owned weapon, not the QC ordered far/mid/close priority lists. (UNCHANGED.); No weapon-combo behaviour (bot_ai_weapon_combo): bots don't hold a weapon for a follow-up combo shot. (UNCHANGED.); No bot_rangepreference shift, so per-bot 'sniper vs spammer' weapon-range personalities are absent. (UNCHANGED.)

[bot-ai.weapon.wr_aim | logic] Per-weapon fire driver wr_aim (secondary fire / detonate / combo / charge)  -  Only the ChosenWeapon's wr_aim is consulted — there is no MAX_WEAPONSLOTS per-slot loop (Base runs wr_aim for every owned weapon slot each think). (UNCHANGED.); wr_aim is ported on 12 of Base's 18 wr_aim weapons; still missing: mortar (airburst), hagar (burst), vortex (charge), seeker, hlac, blaster. (NARROWED 2026-06-27 from 8/18; electro combo + porto fire-veto + crylink/arc pick are now ported.); lastfiredweapon combo bookkeeping is not modeled; combos that depend on the previously-fired weapon don't trigger.

[bot-ai.roles.goalrating | logic,values] Roles + goal rating (havocbot_role_generic / objective roles, routerating)  -  Goal rating still uses straight-line inverse distance (f*rangebias/(rangebias+vlen(dist))) instead of the QC waypoint-cost (Dijkstra) field; bots can prefer a goal near in a straight line but far by path. (UNCHANGED.); rangebias is still hardcoded 2000 for every rate call (items/enemies/roam) instead of the per-rating-call bias QC uses. (UNCHANGED.); No separate _movingtarget (5.5s) strategy interval for goals that can move. (UNCHANGED.); enemyplayers aggression refinement: QC skill>3 nudges the t factor by Strength/Shield powerup timers (StatusEffects_gettime); the port has no status-effect-expiry accessor yet, so that refinement is deferred.

[bot-ai.move.steer | logic] Goal-stack steering + obstacle/step jump (havocbot_movetogoal)  -  QC's full movetogoal handling of teleport/ladder/box-volume waypoints, water swim-up, and the per-goal stop/turn logic is partially collapsed; complex waypoint traversal is less robust than stock. (UNCHANGED.); BotTracewalk (reachability test, separate from the brain danger check) still uses a hardcoded JumpStepHeight const = 48 rather than the live derivation; non-default physics doesn't adjust that one path. (Minor.)

[bot-ai.move.danger | logic] Danger look-ahead (lava/void/cliff/trigger_hurt) + evade  -  Danger evade is still brake-only: the QC lateral evadedanger vector (steer along the safe edge, distance scaled by skill) is collapsed into a reverse-velocity brake, so bots back straight off a ledge instead of sidestepping along it. (UNCHANGED.); Per-waypoint danger-cost ROUTING is inert: a Waypoint.Danger field exists and A* adds nb.Danger, but no botframe_updatedangerousobjects producer ever SETS Danger (always 0), so routing still does not avoid chronically dangerous waypoints — the consumer is dead. (UNCHANGED.)

[bot-ai.combat.dodge | logic,values,timing] Combat movement (projectile dodge + retreat)  -  Only 3 port projectiles flag Entity.BotDodge (Blaster bolt, Porto grenade, PhaserTurret beam) vs Base's ~20+ producers, so most incoming projectiles still aren't dodged even by a SUPERBOT. (NEW RESIDUAL after the dodge port.); Non-SUPERBOT bots still only do the generic CombatMovement strafe-flip + HP-bias (an invented behaviour), not a per-projectile dodge — Base also restricts havocbot_dodge to SUPERBOT, so this matches stock, but the generic strafe is not a Base behaviour. (UNCHANGED.); The SUPERBOT random-direction combat jitter (havocbot_movetogoal:1280-1307) is not reproduced. (UNCHANGED.)

[bot-ai.special.jetpack_rocketjump | logic,values,timing] Jetpack navigation + rocketjump/jetpack trigger_hurt escape  -  Jetpack navigation (take off / fly to point / brake-land) is missing: bots can't cross jetpack-only gaps even with bot_ai_navigation_jetpack on. (UNCHANGED.); Skill>6 trigger_hurt escape (jetpack up, or Devastator rocketjump if HP allows) is missing: bots die in hurt volumes they could have escaped. (UNCHANGED.)

[bot-ai.scripting.commands | logic,values,timing] Bot scripting command queue (bot_cmd / scripting.qc)  -  Bot scripting entirely missing: the bot_cmd command queue does nothing, so map/console bot scripts, cutscene-driven bots, and g_waypointeditor bot driving are inert. (UNCHANGED.)

[bot-waypoints.graph.autolink | liveness] Auto-relink (waypoint_think): PVS + distance + bidirectional tracewalk  -  The it.SUPPORT_WP incoming-direction forbid (waypoints.qc:1213/1227) is not ported — the port has no SUPPORT_WP back-pointer field, so a SUPPORT waypoint forbids only its OWN outgoing/incoming links, not links INTO an arbitrary node it supports. Documented at Waypoint.cs:335. Edge case (SUPPORT waypoints are rare); affects only those specific pairs.

[bot-waypoints.file.save | liveness] Save .waypoints/.cache/.hardwired writers (waypoint_saveall/_save_links/_save_hardwiredlinks)  -  DEAD scaffolding: the three writers (SaveToText/SaveLinksToText/SaveHardwiredLinksToText, Waypoint.cs:880-945) are a faithful inverse of the loaders — version/symmetry/time header, generated-node skip, vtos 1-decimal formatting, the JUMP/SUPPORT/CUSTOM_JP source filter for cache vs hardwired/special sections — BUT have ZERO live callers (grep 2026-06-27: only referenced inside Waypoint.cs). No editor, save command, or test invokes them, so authored graphs still cannot be re-saved on the live path.; Hardwired-vs-special partitioning uses the CustomJp source flag as the hardwired marker (no dedicated wphwXX mirror), so a CUSTOM_JP source's links appear in both the HARDWIRED and SPECIAL sections, matching the QC overlap but losing the precise per-link hardwi

[bot-waypoints.auto.spawnforitem | liveness] Auto item/spawn waypoints (waypoint_spawnforitem)  -  Only runs in the no-.waypoints-file auto-generate path; on maps that ship a .waypoints file the port does NOT inject runtime item/teleporter waypoints (Base always does waypoint_spawnforitem at item spawn, snapping to the item). This is the liveness-partial: the fileless auto-generate fallback is a port-only playability helper, not a Base feature, but it cannot replace Base's always-on runtime injection on shipped maps.

[bot-waypoints.auto.spawnforteleporter | logic,values,liveness] Auto teleporter/jumppad/warpzone waypoints (waypoint_spawnforteleporter/_wz)  -  No warpzone-angle math (Base makevectors(warpzone_angles) src/dest projection) and no oblique-warpzone JUMP flag; jumppad push-time cost is approximated (Base uses trigger_push_get_push_time / the auto-link cost), the port uses the plain travel cost.; Generated-graph path only; shipped maps use authored teleport waypoints from the file.

[bot-waypoints.path.findnearest | logic] Nearest-waypoint query (navigation_findnearestwaypoint)  -  No jumppad-fallback: Base's navigation_findnearestwaypoint tail (navigation.qc:1000, IL_EACH(g_jumppads)) returns a reachable jumppad's nearest waypoint when no plain waypoint is reachable; the port has no such fallback.

[bot-waypoints.path.routetogoal | logic] Route building onto the goal stack (navigation_routetogoal + goalstack)  -  Teleport-goal forcing (push wp00 destination, set goalentity), dynamic/moving-target shortcut (bot_ai_strategyinterval_movingtarget 5.5) and the item incoming-link walkability cache are simplified/absent; the port pushes the A* node chain + goal directly. The path-optimization shortcut (drop the nearest node when the second is a shorter straight shot, BotNavigation.cs:241-250) IS ported.

[bot-waypoints.steer.movetogoal | logic] Steering / path follow (havocbot_movetogoal: jump/crouch/ladder/obstacle/brake/bunnyhop)  -  Obstacle avoidance is a single forward step/jump-height tracebox probe rather than Base's steerlib trident/funnel sweep (see bot-waypoints.steer.steerlib); lateral evade is folded into a straight -velocity brake (do_break), a documented simplification of Base's AI_STATUS_DANGER_AHEAD sidestep.

[bot-waypoints.steer.bunnyhop | logic] Bunnyhop maintenance (havocbot_bunnyhop)  -  Base forbids bunnyhop when evadedanger || do_break is set this frame (havocbot.qc:1315). The port reports bunnyhop intent via a SEPARATE WantBunnyhop flag (BotNavigation.cs:112/378) gated by the Steer-internal ledge brake, then the BotBrain ANDs it with 'no danger brake this frame' before pressing jump — so the brain owns the final danger-suppression. The two-stage gate is close to Base but not a single in-place check.

[bot-waypoints.steer.steerlib | logic,values] steerlib primitives (arrive/attract/repel/flock/swarm/traceavoid/beamsteer/wander)  -  No steerlib library: flocking/swarm/traceavoid/beamsteer primitives absent (group flocking, the trident/funnel traceavoid obstacle steering, and beamsteer). Bots do not flock and use the simpler single-probe avoidance in Steer + the BotDanger fall probe.

[bot-waypoints.editor | logic,values,timing,presentation] Waypoint editor (spawn/remove from editor, symmetry, hardwire, autowaypoints)  -  No in-game waypoint editor: cannot place/remove/hardwire/mirror waypoints (g_waypointeditor*); no editor visualization (waypoint model, link lightning beams, colormod by flag).; No runtime auto-waypointing toward players (botframe_autowaypoints) or useless-waypoint pruning (botframe_deleteuselesswaypoints): the loaded graph is static.

[cl-crosshair.core.master_gating | logic] Master gating + enable/size/alpha early-outs  -  Port gates only on Visible + crosshair_enabled/size/alpha/number; Base's full suppress set (scoreboard/camera/intermission2/game-stopped/lockview/spectatee==-1/HEALTH<=0/DrawCrosshair mutator hook/viewloc/minigame menu/CAMERA_SPECTATOR==2/hud!=HUD_NORMAL) is reduced to the NetGame Visible toggle (havePlayer at NetGame:3366).

[cl-crosshair.core.origin_chase | logic,values,presentation] Crosshair origin + third-person chase trace & body-alpha fade  -  Crosshair is always drawn at the geometric panel centre (DrawPanel: center = Size2*0.5f). The chase-camera branch (trace from player org along view_forward MOVE_WORLDONLY, project the impact, fade the player body alpha toward crosshair_chase_playeralpha while it obstructs the view, restore on chase-off via the crosshair_chase_state latch) is not implemented.; crosshair_chase / crosshair_chase_playeralpha / crosshair_2d are now REGISTERED in RegisterDefaults (CrosshairPanel.cs:323-325) with the Base default values, but have NO reader — they are inert settable cvars (the chase trace + body-alpha fade + side-scroller selection are still unported).

[cl-crosshair.trueaim.showimpact | logic,presentation] Debug: draw crosshair at the reprojected impact point (crosshair_hittest_showimpact)  -  Base (crosshair.qc:306-321): the hit-test block reprojects the box-trace endpoint into screen space (wcross_origin) and, unless crosshair_hittest_showimpact is set, restores wcross_origin to the geometric centre. With showimpact=1 the crosshair is left drawn AT the projected impact point (a debug/aim-assist cue). The port always draws at the geometric panel centre (DrawPanel: center = Size2*0.5f) and never reprojects — the cvar is registered with the Base default (0) but has no reader.; Tied to the same missing screen-space reprojection as the trueaim HITOBSTRUCTION test; default 0 so the visible default behaviour matches Base.

[cl-crosshair.color.special_modes | logic,presentation] Crosshair color: weapon / health+armor / fixed (crosshair_color_special)  -  crosshair_color_special 3 (rainbow: randomvec()*brightness re-rolled every rainbow_delay, crosshair.qc:189-196) is not ported — ColorModeCvar (CrosshairPanel.cs:537-542) maps only 0/1/2, so mode 3 silently falls back to fixed color. The rainbow_brightness/delay cvars are registered but dead.

[cl-crosshair.anim.pickup_pulse | liveness] Item-pickup crosshair pulse (crosshair_pickup)  -  FIXED (host path): UpdatePickupFeed now calls CrosshairPanel.PulsePickup() (NetGame:2864) when a new weapon or a resource jump is detected on the LocalServerPlayer (re-baselined on respawn so the spawn loadout isn't counted). The crosshair bumps on item pickup on the host/listen-server. Still host-only: the detection reads LocalServerPlayer, so a pure remote client (LocalServerPlayer==null) gets no pulse — the networked STAT(LAST_PICKUP) equivalent is not read back into the panel client-side.; Pickup trigger is derived (new-weapon-owned or per-frame resource jump > threshold) rather than the Base STAT(LAST_PICKUP) tick; approximate but matches the visible cue for the host player.

[cl-crosshair.anim.hit_indication | liveness] Hit-indication scale bump + color flash (crosshair_hitindication)  -  Host-only: HitFlash=1 is set only inside `if (_server is not null)` (NetGame:2532, driven by the host-side damagetext mutator drain). A pure remote client gets no hit-indication bump/flash even though HitDamageDealtTotal is networked (ServerNet.cs) and the client diffs it for the hit SOUND — but not for the crosshair flash.; Coupled to the damagetext mutator events rather than the Base path (UpdateDamage diffs STAT(HITSOUND_DAMAGE_DEALT_TOTAL) on STAT(HIT_TIME) advance); fires on local-player-dealt damage only (ev.Attacker==localPlayer && ev.Target!=localPlayer).

[cl-crosshair.anim.smooth_ease | presentation] Goal-based smooth scale/alpha/color ease (crosshair_effect_time)  -  Color is NOT eased: Base eases scale, alpha AND color toward goal over effect_time (crosshair.qc:444 `wcross_color = f*wcross_color + (1-f)*wcross_color_prev`); the port's SmoothGoal eases only scale+alpha and sets color directly each frame. Visually equivalent only for slow color changes within the effect_time window.

[cl-crosshair.anim.switch_crossfade | liveness] Weapon-switch crosshair image cross-fade  -  Keyed on the resolved Texture2D changing rather than the wcross_name/resolution string pair; with the per-weapon dict unpopulated the trigger only fires on a `crosshair` cvar change, NOT on every weapon switch — so in normal play (one crosshair number for all weapons) the cross-fade effectively never runs.

[cl-crosshair.trueaim.teammate_blur_signal | presentation] Teammate shrink + wall/teammate blur signalling  -  Blur is a 5x5 spread of 0.04-alpha texture copies (matches Base's CROSSHAIR_DO_BLUR for textured crosshairs, crosshair.qc:585-596); only applies to the textured path (DrawCrosshairTexture), not the vector fallback (DrawVectorCrosshair).

[cl-crosshair.ring.vortex_charge | liveness] Vortex / Overkill-Nex charge ring (+ inner chargepool / moving-average ring)  -  FIXED (host path): UpdateCrosshairWeaponRings now feeds CrosshairPanel.ChargeFraction = st.VortexCharge and .ChargePool = st.VortexChargePoolAmmo (NetGame:3153-54) while a vortex/vaporizer/*nex* weapon is active off the host-side slot-0 WeaponSlotState (the C# home of QC wepent.*). The Vortex charge ring + inner chargepool/moving-average ring now render for the host player. VortexCharge is genuinely produced server-side (Vortex.cs/OkNex.cs). Still host-only: read off LocalServerPlayer inside `if (_server is not null)`; a pure remote client (LocalServerPlayer==null) gets the -1 sentinel and no ring.; Minor fidelity: ChargePool is fed unconditionally for vortex-like weapons (default VortexChargePoolAmmo=1f), so the inner chargepool ring shows even when secondary cha

[cl-crosshair.ring.reload_ammo | liveness] Reload / ammo (clip) ring (+ Rifle ring art)  -  FIXED (host path): UpdateCrosshairWeaponRings now feeds CrosshairPanel.ClipLoad = st.ClipLoad and .ClipSize = st.ClipSize when the active slot has a clip (NetGame:3165-66). The ammo ring renders for every reloadable weapon on the host. ClipLoad/ClipSize are genuinely maintained on the slot by WeaponFireGate/WeaponFireDriver. Still host-only: read off LocalServerPlayer inside `if (_server is not null)`; remote clients get the -1/0 sentinel and no ring.

[cl-crosshair.ring.minelayer_hagar_arc | liveness] Mine Layer / Hagar load / Arc heat rings  -  FIXED (host path): UpdateCrosshairWeaponRings now feeds CrosshairPanel.HagarLoad (st.HagarLoad, NetGame:3177), .MineCount (live g_mines scan of mines owned by the player, 3189-92), and .ArcHeat (st.BeamHeat/OverheatMax with the overheat-jam latch, 3208-11) when the matching weapon is active. Mine-count, Hagar burst-load, and Arc overheat rings now render for the host player. HagarLoad/BeamHeat/ArcOverheat are genuinely produced server-side (Hagar.cs/Arc.cs). Still host-only: read off LocalServerPlayer inside `if (_server is not null)`; remote clients get the -1 sentinel and no ring.

[cl-crosshair.ring.switch_fade | liveness] Weapon-switch ring fade-out/in (wcross_ring_prev)  -  Now reachable on the host path: with the weapon-stat rings fed (UpdateCrosshairWeaponRings), DrawStatRing produces rings and calls SwitchRingFade (DrawStatRing:943), so the switch fade runs when the host player swaps between ring-bearing weapons. Still dead for a pure remote client (no ring ever draws there).

[cl-crosshair.objring.nade_capture_revive | presentation,liveness] Objective rings: NADE_TIMER > CAPTURE_PROGRESS > REVIVE_PROGRESS  -  PARTIAL FIX: REVIVE_PROGRESS is now fed (NetGame:2559, _fullHud.Crosshair.ReviveProgress = LocalServerPlayer?.ReviveProgress) alongside NadeTimer (2558), both host-only inside `if (_server is not null)`. Player.ReviveProgress is genuinely mirrored each tick by FreezeTag (FreezeTag.cs), so the host player's thaw ring now renders. STILL OPEN: CAPTURE_PROGRESS is never assigned on any production path (grep finds only test-side .CaptureProgress= plus the networked NetEntity.CaptureProgress field which ServerNet.cs explicitly leaves 0 with a comment that no gametype sets it) so the capture ring is DEAD; and although NetEntityState.ReviveProgress IS networked to remote clients (ServerNet.cs), NOTHING reads it back into the panel client-side — a pure remote cli

[cl-crosshair.per_weapon_2d_vehicle | logic,values,presentation,liveness] Per-weapon crosshair pic + 2D side-scroller + vehicle crosshair  -  Per-weapon crosshair: PerWeaponNumber dict is never populated by any caller (grep finds zero assignments outside the property def CrosshairPanel.cs:97), so every weapon shows the same `crosshair` number (Base maps each weapon to its own w_crosshair pic + w_crosshair_size multiplier).; crosshair_2d (side-scroller, default 54) is now REGISTERED (CrosshairPanel.cs:323) but has no reader — the FREEAIM/viewloc side-scroller crosshair selection is unported. HUD_Crosshair_Vehicle (info.vr_crosshair) has no port.

[cl-csqcmodel.predraw.dispatch_order | logic,presentation] Predraw dispatch + ordered pass (appearance->LOD->anim->tag->effects)  -  Order is appearance->LOD->effects (skeletal anim runs in a separate PlayerModel.Pose pass, not interleaved here); the appearance-before-LOD ordering is moot because LOD is a no-op swap.; AutoTagIndex recursion + drawmask=0 on null-model are not part of this pass (held weapons attach via ViewEntityRenderer).

[cl-csqcmodel.skeleton.upper_lower_aim | presentation] Skeletal upper/lower split + aim bones + fixbone  -  Presentation gap is on the ACTION side, not the split mechanism: the upper-body torso always shows a stable idle/aim frame (LocomotionBlend.Split torsoTime=0), so the bone split + fixbone + aim-pitch are structurally faithful but the torso never animates beyond the aim-bone pitch bend (the upper-body ACTION overlay is the animdecide feature's gap).; Aim pitch reads Entity.Angles.X instead of a separately-networked v_angle_x (BIT(10)); for remote players these can differ slightly (the visible head/torso bend may lag or use the body yaw-pitch rather than the true view pitch).

[cl-csqcmodel.animdecide.state_machine | logic,values,timing,presentation,liveness] animdecide locomotion + upper-body action state machine  -  Upper-body ACTIONS still NOT ported: the networked anim_state / anim_upper_action / anim_lower_action / anim_time inputs are not sent or read (grep confirms zero anim_* bits in NetEntity/ClientEntityView), so shoot/melee/pain/draw/taunt torso overlays never play on the third-person model (the torso shows a single stable idle/aim clip, Split torsoTime=0).; Per-anim framerates (die 0.5fps, shoot 5, jump 10, draw 3, taunt 0.33) and the per-clip framegroup names are not used; clips are picked by a fuzzy name Contains match with a 20fps default (BuildClipTable.Pick + FrameGroup(...,20f,...)).; The CSQC-side implicit jump detection (ground->air transition sets ANIMACTION_JUMP) is approximated by the inAir->Jump/DuckJump clip pick; no explicit end-of-ju

[cl-csqcmodel.fallbackframe.remap | liveness] Fallback frame remap (missing-frame substitution)  -  Live only on the MD3 ModelAnimator player path; skeletal-IQM players drive frames via LocomotionBlend, which never produces the high-numbered melee/duckwalk frame ids that the remap targets, so the remap rarely fires in practice.

[cl-csqcmodel.tagindex.weapon_attach | logic,presentation,liveness] Auto tag-index (weapon/attachment bone resolution)  -  The QC h_/v_ model tag resolution cascade (weapon bone -> gettagindex weapon/tag_weapon -> shot/tag_shot, with recursive parent predraw and drawmask=0 when the tag can't resolve) is simplified to a tag_weapon marker attach; weapon-on-weapon and the shot-muzzle tag chain are not faithfully reproduced.; No recursive predraw of the parent entity to fix forcemodel/LOD bone-index mismatch (forcemodel is live, so a forced-model bone-index mismatch on the held weapon attach is a possible edge case; LOD swap is still absent so no LOD-driven mismatch).

[cl-csqcmodel.effects.ef_mf_trail_lights | logic,values,presentation,liveness] EF_*/MF_* effects: dynamic lights, particles, render-flags, trails  -  FIX (Wave 5): the per-player forced-effects seam is now LIVE for MF_ROCKET. ClientWorld.DriveCsqcModelHooks composes ForcedAppearance(jetpackActive:true) from the networked e.UsingJetpack and passes it to CsqcModelEffects.Apply(...,forced); forced.ModelFlags ORs MF_ROCKET into modelFlags, driving both the rocket trail return and the jetpack loop. So MF_ROCKET trail + loop now fire for remote players.; STILL OPEN: the OTHER MF trails (grenade/gib/tracer/zomgib/tracer2/tracer3) are mapped in CsqcModelEffectFlags but never supplied — base modelFlags is still 0 and only the jetpack composes MF_ROCKET, so non-jetpack MF-driven trails never fire on this path.; RECLASSIFIED (role-glow is actually LIVE): the baseline claimed Strength/Shield role-gl

[cl-csqcmodel.networking.csqcmodel_contract | logic,values,presentation,liveness] CSQCMODEL networked-property contract (effects/modelflags/skin/traileffect/colormap/colormod/glowmod/scale/alpha/v_angle/anim_*)  -  FIX (Wave 5): ALPHA is now FULLY wired end-to-end (closed the baseline 'half-wired/dead-on-consume' gap). The producer (ServerNet.QuantizeAlpha for players + entities), codec (WriteByte/ReadByte at EntityField.Alpha 1<<17), the client consume (ClientEntityView: `e.Alpha = s.Alpha == 0 ? 1f : s.Alpha/255f`), and the render (PlayerModel.ApplyAlpha sets per-instance GeometryInstance3D.Transparency on every mesh) are all live. A Cloaked (0.25) / Invisibility / fading player now renders translucent on the client.; FIX (Wave 5): UsingJetpack (IT_USING_JETPACK) is now networked (NetEntityFlags widened byte->ushort, bit 8) and consumed, letting the client re-derive MF_ROCKET (trail +

[cl-hud.engine.registry_and_order | presentation] Panel registry, z-order, panel-order cvar  -  Draw order follows Godot child order, not the QC _hud_panelorder reverse-walk; relative panel layering may differ but panels don't overlap in luma.

[clanarena.hud.eliminated_greyout | logic] Eliminated-player scoreboard grey-out  -  IsEliminatedPlayer == IsDead only; QC ca_isEliminated also greys INGAME_JOINING late joiners and FRAGS_PLAYER_OUT_OF_GAME, which the port still lacks, so a late joiner waiting out the round isn't greyed until they die.

[clanarena.join.late_join_observer | logic,values,timing,presentation] Late joiner forced to Observer until next round (+ CA join-late info)  -  While !allowed_to_spawn, QC forces a joining player to Observer (TRANSMUTE), sets INGAME_STATUS_JOINING, and sends INFO_CA_JOIN_LATE so they play from the next round. (The CA_JOIN_LATE notification IS registered, NotificationsList.cs:702, but never sent — no live caller.); QC Bot_FixCount counts in-game-joined clients (so bots fill mid-round) -- not ported.

[clanarena.matchend.restore_status | logic] Restore spectator/team status before final scores (MatchEnd_BeforeScores)  -  At match end Base restores each player's pre-CA spectator/team status from the .prev_team field (snapshotted in CA_RoundStart) so the post-match scoreboard reflects who was actually playing. The MatchEnd_BeforeScores hook infrastructure now EXISTS in the port (MutatorHooks.MatchEndBeforeScores, fired at GameWorld.cs:3791), but CA does not subscribe to it and snapshots no prev_team, so the final-scores spectator/team restoration is not modeled.

[clanarena.spectate.force_spectate_cmd | logic,presentation] CA spectate-command force + 'leave the game' notice (ClientCommand_Spectate)  -  Base intercepts the `spectate` client command for an INGAME player: it force-spectates (MUT_SPECCMD_FORCE) and, when sv_spectate is on and they're already spec/observer, sends INFO_CA_LEAVE ('left the game'). Tied to the missing INGAME state.

[clanarena.cmd.shuffleteams_reset | logic] shuffleteams reschedules to next round (SV_ParseServerCommand)  -  Base defers a `shuffleteams` command issued mid-round to the next ResetMap (shuffleteams_on_reset_map = !allowed_to_spawn) so a live CA round isn't disrupted. The port has a `shuffleteams` admin command (Commands.cs:627/CmdShuffleTeams) but no CA SV_ParseServerCommand hook to round-defer it, so it shuffles immediately even mid-round.

[clanarena.lifecycle.reset_map | logic] Round reset re-transmutes in-game players and re-allows spawn (reset_map_players/global)  -  QC reset_map_players: for each INGAME-or-bot client, clear killcount, TRANSMUTE(Player), INGAME_STATUS_SET(JOINED), PutClientInServer, reset prev_team. QC reset_map_global: allowed_to_spawn = true. The port re-spawns everyone at round reset via its round-gated DeadPlayerThink/ResetMap machinery (effective one-life-per-round result holds), but has no CA-specific reset_map hook: no INGAME->Player transmute (no INGAME state at all), no killcount reset, no prev_team reset, and no explicit allowed_to_spawn flag (the port gates spawn on round phase instead). Practically equivalent for the common case; the late-joiner/INGAME details are tied to clanarena.join.late_join_observer.

[ctf.match_end_freeze | logic,liveness] Freeze dropped/passing flags at match end (MatchEnd)  -  QC the ctf MatchEnd hook walks g_flags and, for any FLAG_DROPPED/FLAG_PASSING flag, locks it (MOVETYPE_NONE, DAMAGE_NO, SOLID_NOT, nextthink=false) so a live flag cannot move/be-touched after the game is over. The port has the MatchEnded gate (Pickup/Capture/Throw/Pass/Tick early-return when MatchEnded), which stops the STATE MACHINE, but it does not freeze the physical dropped/passing flag ENTITY: a MoveType.Toss dropped flag keeps integrating + its FlagThinkEntity keeps re-arming after MatchEnded.

OnMakePlayerObserver is now ported+live (FRAGS_PLAYER_OUT_OF_GAME when the observer holds a ranked time, else FRAGS_SPECTATOR; AbandonRun + race_checkpoint=-1, Cts.cs:611-623), matching QC. Still MISSING: ClientConnect / PutClientInServer / AbortSpeedrun hooks (race_SendAll on connect, the PutClientInServer spawn==qualifying re-prepare-vs-race_RetractPlayer branch, the g_allow_checkpoints practice-mode re-prepare), and respawn-checkpoint/spotref recording.

[cts.records.getrecords | logic] Map-record listing reply (GetRecords): per-map rank-1 CTS time + holder  -  The port models the single-map ranking listing (QC getrankings: ranks 1..RANKINGS_CNT of the current map) and wires it live to the rankings/records console commands. [deathmatch.scoring.teamkill_punishing | logic,values] Teamkill punishment escalation (g_teamkill_punishing)  -  DM is FFA so this never fires in practice; the escalating teamkill penalty is unmodeled, but unreachable in deathmatch (relevant only to team modes).

[domination.roundbased.player_blocked | logic,liveness] Round-based player-blocking on round start / spawn  -  QC reset_map_players sets it.player_blocked = 1 on map reset (roundbased) and PlayerSpawn sets player.player_blocked = !round_handler_IsRoundStarted(); Domination_RoundStart clears player_blocked = false for all players.
